title: Blyth Green (2) Halifax Memorial
crew_summary: 8 killed including Flt Lt. George Douglas Stark

external_narratives.summary: RCAF 425 (Alouette) Squadron records document Halifax NA581 as lost on 2 August 1944 during a daytime test flight from RAF Tolthorpe, when it suffered engine problems and crashed near Blyth village.
Aviation Archaeology lists Halifax NA581 in its 1944 military crash records, confirming the crash near Blyth on 2 August 1944 with crew casualties including Canadian airmen P/O Pigeon and P/O Pitkin.
key_details: P/O John Jerome Pigeon from Stratford, Ontario killed
P/O Edmund Francis Pitkin from Toronto killed

external_narratives.summary: The Nottinghamshire Aviation Memorials guide includes the Blyth village green memorial, erected 14 September 1997, which commemorates crews of both Wellington HE818 and Halifax NA581.
key_details: Shared memorial covers both HE818 and NA581 crews

local_contacts.role: Local authority responsible for memorial upkeep

sources: OSM node 3213929783 Wellington bomber crash memorial (53.1902622 -0.9140349)

UNCERTAIN. Previous coords (53.172176 -0.909788) in Laxton/Moorhouse area. OSM has Wellington crash memorial 2km north at 53.190 -0.914 but labelled as Wellington not Halifax. Possible coordinates swap with blyth-green-1-he818 — see that entry. Corrected to nearest OSM memorial node as best estimate but confidence is low. Physical verification required.
